name: Brynja Ashbow

title: Ashbow — Fey Wanderer Ranger Sheet

paras: Brynja Ashbow was raised among the Hrafnir, a people of trackers and outriders who range the wooded marches where the mortal world thins against the Feywild.

As a child she wandered too far along a deer trail and crossed, without knowing it, into the realm beyond. She came back a day later by her own reckoning — a full season by everyone else's — carrying a quiet that never quite left her.
The fey took a liking to her. Their glamour clings to her still: strangers trust her too quickly, animals come when she calls, and a faint silver shimmer haunts the edge of her sight.
She works now as a guide and hunter for hire, following trails others lose. The wilds hold few secrets from her, and she has learned to turn the fey's gifts of charm and misdirection to her own ends.
Lately the boundaries have grown thin again, and things are crossing over that should not. Brynja means to find out why — and to be the one waiting when they do.

items.html: Species: Human — Resourceful, Skillful (+1 skill), Versatile (+1 Origin feat), Speed 30
Ability scores: Str 8, Dex 17, Con 13, Int 10, Wis 15, Cha 12 (standard array 15/14/13/12/10/8; background +2 Dex / +1 Wis)
Favored Enemy: Hunter's Mark always prepared, free casts = proficiency / long rest
Background: Guide — Origin feat Magic Initiate (Druid); skills Stealth & Survival; Cartographer's Tools; +2 Dex / +1 Wis
Magic Initiate (Druid): Guidance & Message cantrips, Speak with Animals (Wis casting)
Human Versatile: Origin feat → Lucky (2 Luck Points · Advantage or impose Disadvantage)
Fighting Style: Defense (+1 AC while wearing armor)
Dreadful Strikes — +1d4 psychic on a weapon hit
Otherworldly Glamour: +Wis to Charisma checks · proficiency in Persuasion
Ability Score Improvement → Archery feat — +2 to ranged weapon attack rolls (the longbow's +7)

longbow.title: Longbow
body: One attack on your turn (Extra Attack arrives at level 5). +7 to hit = Dex +3, proficiency +2, and the Archery feat +2. Damage adds your Dexterity (+3).
Slow (mastery): when you hit and deal damage, the target's Speed drops by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
Needs ammunition and two free hands to fire.

shortsword.title: Shortsword
body: Your melee sidearm. Finesse lets you use Dexterity for attack and damage, so +5 to hit (Dex +3, proficiency +2) and 1d6+3.
Vex (mastery): when you hit and deal damage, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that target, until the end of your next turn.

dreadful.title: Dreadful Strikes
body: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a weapon, you can deal an extra 1d4 psychic damage. Each creature can take this extra damage only once per turn.
It costs no action and stacks with Hunter's Mark; the die grows to 1d6 at ranger level 11.
It isn't magical and needs no Concentration — it simply rides your hits.

favoredenemy.title: Favored Enemy
body: You always have Hunter's Mark prepared, and you can cast it without a spell slot a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus (2) per long rest. After that, cast it with a slot.
This is your signature damage rider — turn it on as a Bonus Action at the start of a fight.

deftexplorer.title: Deft Explorer
body: You gain Expertise in one skill — taken in Insight, doubling your Proficiency Bonus there (Insight +6). You also learn two additional languages (Goblin and Undercommon).
Move the Expertise onto another proficient skill if your table prefers.

glamour.title: Otherworldly Glamour
body: Whenever you make a Charisma check, add your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1) — here +2. You also gain proficiency in one of Deception, Performance, or Persuasion — taken in Persuasion.
This makes a Charisma-12 ranger a strong talker: Persuasion is +5 (Cha +1, prof +2, glamour +2).

bootsofelvenkind.title: Boots of Elvenkind
body: While you wear these boots, your steps make no sound, regardless of the surface you move across.
You also have Advantage on any Dexterity (Stealth) check you make that relies on moving silently.

defense.title: Fighting Style: Defense
body: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC. Brynja wears studded leather, so her AC is 16 (12 + Dex 3 + Defense 1).

coreranger.title: Core Ranger Traits
body: You have a d10 Hit Die, proficiency with simple and martial weapons, light and medium armor and shields, and Strength and Dexterity saving throws. You also choose your Ranger skills from the class list.

rangerspell.title: Spellcasting
body: You cast Ranger spells using Wisdom: spell save DC 12, spell attack +4. At level 4 you have three 1st-level spell slots, regained on a long rest. Prepare Ranger spells after a long rest; always-prepared spells (Hunter's Mark, Charm Person) don't count against the limit.

feywanderer.title: Fey Wanderer
body: A ranger whose travels brushed the Feywild. You gain Otherworldly Glamour, Dreadful Strikes, and always-prepared Fey Wanderer spells, plus a fey blessing from the Feywild Gifts table.

feywanderspells.title: Fey Wanderer Spells
body: You always have Charm Person prepared (it doesn't count against your prepared total); more Fey spells are added as you level. You also possess a fey blessing — choose it from the Feywild Gifts table or roll for it.

archery.title: Archery (Fighting Style feat)
body: Taken with the level-4 Ability Score Improvement (Fighting Style feats need the Fighting Style feature, which Rangers gain at level 2). You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls with ranged weapons — already included in the longbow's +7 to hit.
Your level-2 Fighting Style is Defense (+1 AC while wearing armor); you can't choose the same Fighting Style twice, so Archery is a separate second style.

grantsPool.label: Hunter's Mark — free casts
grantsPool.reminder: Hunter's Mark: free cast (no slot). Bonus Action; +1d6 force on each hit.

hitRiders.label: Dreadful Strikes
hitRiders.note: psychic · once per turn, no action
